title: Poa pseudoaltaica
taxonomy: Poa pseudoaltaica is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Poa and is commonly known as a type of meadow grass. Poa pseudoaltaica is native to mountainous regions and is often found in high-altitude areas. The species is closely related to other Poa species, such as Poa alpina and Poa chaixii. Poa pseudoaltaica is typically classified as a perennial grass, growing from a rhizome or a short stem.

intro: Poa pseudoaltaica, also known as Pseudoaltaica Meadow Grass, is a type of grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Poa pseudoaltaica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Poa pseudoaltaica is often made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is also used to make medicinal tea and powder for the treatment of various health conditions.
